title: Remove search/filter feature from Dashboard

requirements.text: Remove the search/filter input and all associated filtering logic from the public-facing Dashboard page (frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx). Delete any search state, filter handlers, and keyword-matching code that filters incidents by title, affected system/service, or description. The Dashboard should display all active and resolved incidents directly without any filtering controls. Both the active and resolved incident sections must render their full lists unconditionally.

quotes: Ok lets get rid of the search/filter feature. I think thats what broke this

acceptanceCriteria: The Dashboard page contains no search input or filter controls
No search/filter state variables exist in the Dashboard component
All active incidents are displayed without any keyword filtering applied
All resolved incidents are displayed without any keyword filtering applied
The 'no results' message tied to the search feature is removed
Removing the search feature does not introduce any new console errors or rendering issues

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Do not alter backend logic or the Motoko actor
Do not remove the delete button for resolved incidents in the Admin Panel
Do not remove the light/dark mode toggle from the Layout header
nonGoals: Adding any new filtering or search functionality
Changing the visual theme or layout beyond removing the search UI
